thesis: Nebius is a transformational AI infrastructure play with two of the largest compute contracts in history (Meta $27B, Microsoft $19.4B). Revenue grew 351% in FY2025 to $530M with improving margins and $2.45B cash. However, the stock trades at 75x P/S, RSI is 85 (extremely overbought), and customer concentration risk is high. A SPEC. BUY for investors who can stomach extreme volatility and believe AI capex demand remains durable.

drivers: Meta and Microsoft contracts execute on schedule with no delays
Margin expansion to 40%+ as operating leverage kicks in
Vera Rubin and Blackwell deployments establish Nebius as tier-1 AI infrastructure provider

risks: Deployment delays push revenue recognition into 2027–2028
GPU supply constraints or cost overruns compress margins
Meta or Microsoft slows AI capex, reducing contracted take rates
Multiple compression from 75x P/S to 15–20x as growth decelerates

base_case.assumptions: Core contracts deliver on schedule with minor delays. 2026 revenue $2.5–$3.0B in line with Goldman estimates. AI Cloud platform attracts enough third-party customers to reduce concentration risk. Margins improve but remain below long-term targets due to ongoing capex intensity.

catalysts.event: Q1 2026 Earnings — first report post-Meta deal, ARR update
Blackwell GPU deployment milestones — capacity expansion progress
Vera Rubin delivery to Meta — first revenue from largest contract

key_risks: Extreme customer concentration (Meta + Microsoft = majority of forward revenue)
Valuation risk at 75x P/S and 1,366x P/E — priced for perfection
Execution risk at scale — data center build-outs are technically complex and capital-intensive
Geopolitical headline risk from Yandex/Russian roots
